name: Taiwan (TSMC)
summary: Home to TSMC — manufactures ~90% of the world's most advanced semiconductors. Helium supply disruption from Qatar threatens fab operations. TAIEX at 33,113 (-6.5% monthly). TSMC's exposure is indirect (helium → fab coolant/process gas → production capacity) but systemically critical. If TSMC reduces output, AI infrastructure and global tech valuations take a direct hit.

signals.content: TAIEX at 33,113 (-0.7% daily, -6.5% monthly). TSMC has not publicly commented on helium supply impact but industry sources report allocation tightening. SMH (semiconductor ETF) down 9.2% monthly. If helium shortage forces TSMC to reduce advanced node production, cascade hits AI infrastructure (Nvidia, AMD, cloud providers) and tech valuations globally. (Sources: yfinance, gasworld, industry analysis)

edges.context: TSMC uses helium as process gas and coolant in advanced chip fabrication. Qatar helium disruption threatens production capacity at world's most critical semiconductor facility.
TSMC supplies chips to Apple, Nvidia, AMD, Qualcomm — the largest S&P 500 components by market cap. Production disruption cascades directly to US tech valuations.
TSMC and Samsung compete in advanced semiconductor fabrication. Both exposed to same helium supply chain from Qatar.

trigger_points.condition: TSMC announces production reduction or allocation changes due to helium/gas supply
trigger_points.mechanism: TSMC producing 90% of advanced chips means any production cut cascades immediately to every tech company worldwide. This would shift the crisis from 'energy shock' to 'tech supply crisis.'
